issue_title: Enforce the `Content-Type` header for routes with payload
issue_body:
Checked with @gmourier: we will not open any specification for this since the changes are minimal to detail and make the current behavior more consistent instead of integrating a new feature.

### Goal

Make the routes (accepting a payload) check if a `Content-Type` header has been defined. If it's not, MeiliSearch should return a `415 Unsupported Media Type` error:

```
{
    "message": "A Content-Type header is missing. Accepted values for Content-Type are: :contentTypeList",
    "code": "missing_content_type",
    "type": "invalid_request",
    "link": "https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#missing_content_type"
}
```

### Why?

- The current behavior of MeiliSearch is inconsistent: the `POST /indexes/:uid/settings` and `POST /indexes/:uid/search` routes returns a `415` error when no `Content-Type` header are set, but the `POST /indexes/:uid/documents` route does not and infer the type as JSON.
- This enforces more precision and the user to avoid mistakes: there are aware there are sending a JSON
- This is not really impactful for most of the users: the users using SDKs will not see the change since the SDKs send a `Content-Type: application/json` by default.

### Impact for the docs

@meilisearch/docs-team 

The documentation needs to be updated:
- the `POST`/`PUT` examples (for the `cURL` tab) that need a payload must add the following line: `-H 'Content-Type: application/json'`
⚠️ The `POST /dumps` must not enforce the `Content-Type` header since no payload is passed.
- we also recommend updating all the docs examples using `--data-binary` instead of `--data`: we ensure the data are not interpreted by `cURL`, which is not the case with `--data`.
Here is the description of `--data-binary` in `man curl`: "(HTTP) This posts data exactly as specified with no extra processing whatsoever."

----

### TODO

MeiliSearch should return `415 Unsupported Media Type` when:
-  no `Content-Type` header is defined
- or if it is defined with an invalid media type

Here are the concerned routes:
  - `POST indexes/`
  - `PUT indexes/:uid`
  - `POST indexes/:uid/documents`
  - `POST indexes/:uid/documents/delete-batch`
  - `PUT indexes/:uid/documents`
  - `POST indexes/:uid/search`
  - `POST indexes/:uid/settings`
  - all the sub-settings routes like `POST indexes/:uid/settings/XXX`

⚠️ The `POST /dumps` must not enforce the `Content-Type` header since no payload is passed.
pr_title: Enforce `Content-Type` header for routes requiring a body payload
